title: Steven Spielberg Reveals if Elliott and E.T. Ever Reunited After Sci-Fi Epic

blocks.content: There is some intense buzz surrounding Steven Spielberg right now, owing to the recent release of his newest big-screen blockbuster, Disclosure Day, which marks the filmmaker's highly anticipated return to the genre that defined his career. His latest movie is widely considered the culmination of a thematic trilogy alongside Close Encounters of the Third Kind and E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial, aiming to confront some of the uncertainties and unresolved matters left behind by those earlier cinematic classics. However, one big question still lingers: What exactly happened to Elliott and E.T. after their adventures in the '80s sci-fi romp?

E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial chronicles the epic journey between a lonely boy named Elliott and a stranded alien known as E.T., capturing everything from their first meeting in the backyard of a quiet California suburb to their budding friendship, shifting home dynamics and subsequent "Phone Home" mission, leading to the iconic high-stakes bicycle escape to return E.T. to the mothership that has landed in the forest. Their adventure concludes with a heartbreaking but hopeful farewell, where E.T. touches his glowing finger on Elliott's forehead and says, "I'll be right here," promising that their telepathic and emotional bond will endure.

The touching exchange between the pair prompted decades of fan theories about Elliott and E.T.'s close-knit bond, and what happened after the spaceship departed Earth. During a recent appearance on Josh Horowitz's Happy Sad Confused podcast, Spielberg finally quelled speculation and revealed the definitive truth about the aftermath of his sci-fi epic. The director answered the long-standing questions that have plagued minds by stating that Elliott "never saw" E.T. again – at least, in a physical sense. He asserted that the beloved duo remained linked across space and time due to their unbreakable psychic connection, explaining:

"Never saw him again. But he did dream about him. So, there was the psychic link between the two of them. If you notice that E.T. touched Elliott right here and said, "I'll be right here." That was for the rest of Elliott's life."

Fans will recall that Elliott did in fact reunite with E.T. in a 2019 Xfinity holiday commercial titled "A Holiday Reunion," in which Henry Thomas reprised his role as Elliott, now as an adult with a wife and two children of his own, to welcome E.T. to his suburban home and show him how technology has advanced. While many viewed the advertisement as a sequel (of sorts) to the 1982 feature, Spielberg's latest comments confirm that the short should not be viewed as official film canon. Within the narrative universe of the original movie, the physical goodbye in the forest was Elliott and E.T.'s final real-world interaction – as painful as it is to admit.
